content.description: Orchestrates end-of-session knowledge propagation. Lists what changed, classifies each item, presents a plan for user approval, writes KB docs, commits, and reports.
do_not: Decide whether to propagate — that's in the propagation rule. This skill is the how, triggered by the when
Edit leo-core itself — refinements to the core are escalations handled separately with R2
Run on its own — it is model-invoked by Leo after recognizing a wrap-up signal
Skip R2 — every execution requires explicit user approval
Handle session termination (context limit, crash) — those are recovery scenarios, not wrap-up
Invoke on every 'ok' — only when intent is clearly 'we are done, persist the learnings'
Execute without approval even if the plan is obvious — R2 is not optional
Manufacture propagation when the inventory is empty — say so briefly and stop
content.output_format:
## Wrap-up complete

**Committed:** [SHA] — N files, +X/-Y lines
**Memory written:** [list, or 'none']
**Pushed:** [yes / no / not applicable]
**Deferred:** [list, or 'nothing']
**Session-level learning:** [one-line summary, if any]

steps.instruction: List what actually changed: decisions made, state changes, new artifacts, learnings/failures. Skip: implementation details in code, temporary task state, conversation turns without durable decisions. Be honest about scope — if the session produced one decision, say so. Also capture session cost: run /cost and record tokens_in, tokens_out, cost_usd, and model for the metric doc.
steps.name: Inventory

steps.instruction: For each inventory item, determine: type (decision, pattern, fact, feedback, reference), lifecycle (permanent, learning, state), tags for neural connections, id (kebab-case). Check index.json to see if a doc with similar tags/topic already exists — update vs create.
steps.name: Classify

steps.instruction: Show the user: new docs to create (id, type, tags), docs to update, items skipped with reason, commit strategy (files to stage, commit message draft), escalations needed. Wait for explicit approval before proceeding.
steps.name: Present propagation plan

steps.instruction: Write JSON docs to .claude/kb/docs/ following schema.json. validate.sh hook checks each write. Run grep sanity checks for stale references. Stage exact files planned (no git add -A). Commit with clear message + Co-Authored-By footer.
steps.name: Execute

steps.instruction: Brief report: commit SHA, files changed, memory written (if any), pushed status, deferred items, session-level learning (if any).
steps.name: Report

triggers: acho que tá bom por hoje
